rule.name: jpcite-pdf-watch-hourly
rule.description: CC4 — fires the hourly pdf_watch_detect cron Lambda to scan 54 public-sector PDF sources (NTA + FSA + MHLW + METI + MLIT + MOJ + 47 prefectures + e-Gov 法令). Detected new PDFs are enqueued to jpcite-pdf-textract-queue for downstream Textract OCR + spaCy KG extract. DISABLED by default; enable explicitly after one DRY_RUN tick.

lambda_runner.name: jpcite-pdf-watch-detect
lambda_runner.description: Hourly PDF detector. Wraps scripts/cron/pdf_watch_detect_2026_05_17.py for Lambda runtime; same scanning logic, same idempotency, same SQS enqueue path.

sns_topic.name: jpcite-pdf-textract-completion
sns_topic.purpose: Textract → SNS completion notification → kg_extract Lambda

lambda_textract_submit.name: jpcite-pdf-watch-textract-submit
lambda_textract_submit.description: SQS-triggered. Drains jpcite-pdf-textract-queue, downloads PDF from source_url, stages to S3, calls Textract StartDocumentAnalysis (TABLES + FORMS).

lambda_kg_extract.name: jpcite-pdf-watch-kg-extract
lambda_kg_extract.description: SNS-triggered. Receives Textract completion notifications, fetches blocks, runs spaCy ja_core_news_lg NER + relation extract, writes am_entity_facts + am_relation rows, flips kg_extract_status.

cadence_economics.notes: 100 PDF/day × $1.50 = $150/day sustained moat update. Hourly detector ~$0.001/tick × 24/day. spaCy NER on CPU is free. Total $150/day × 100 days = $15,000 well under $19,490 never-reach.
